claim: Fixed floral orientation can alter morning warming and pollinator visitation
allowed_wording: orientation can alter floral temperature and visitor behaviour
prohibited_wording: orientation evolved to attract pollinators in Cirsium
remaining_test: Angle manipulation plus thermal and visitation monitoring in Cirsium

claim: Floral orientation can modify solar loading and gynoecium temperature
allowed_wording: orientation changes radiative and thermal exposure
prohibited_wording: erect or nodding heads are universally warmer
remaining_test: Temperature logging across manipulated angles and climates

claim: Sun-facing flowers can create warm floral microhabitats
allowed_wording: orientation may mediate thermal interactions in cold environments
remaining_test: Replicate in thistle capitula across climates
Simulated rainfall crossed with angle manipulation and pollen nectar seed-set measures

claim: Flower-colour transitions can involve anthocyanin pathway changes
allowed_wording: colour evolution can involve pigment-pathway regulation
prohibited_wording: image hue identifies a mutation or pigment amount

claim: Human-visible hue incompletely represents hymenopteran colour
best_support: Chittka 1992; Chittka et al. 1994; Briscoe and Chittka 2001
allowed_wording: camera colour is not pollinator colour
remaining_test: Calibrated UV-visible spectra and receptor-noise models

claim: Floral colour can guide pollinator decisions
allowed_wording: colour can function as information for visitors
prohibited_wording: a particular thistle hue is universally preferred
remaining_test: Choice experiments with local visitor guilds

allowed_wording: environmental colour structure is physiologically plausible

claim: Soil can influence flower colour in special chemical systems
allowed_wording: edaphic effects are plausible and system dependent
remaining_test: Tissue elemental analysis pigment chemistry and controlled soils

claim: Two-dimensional outline combines biology and viewpoint
allowed_wording: shape metrics quantify visible form under defined QC
remaining_test: Multi-view imaging repeated developmental stages and segmentation audit

claim: Traditional generic boundaries can conflict with molecular clades
allowed_wording: the group has unresolved systematic structure

claim: Cytotype differences can reveal overlooked lineages
allowed_wording: ploidy and morphology may identify distinct evolutionary units
remaining_test: Flow cytometry chromosome counts and population genomics

claim: Contemporary hybridization can blur lineage boundaries
prohibited_wording: hybridization caused weak signal in this study

claim: Cirsium is a rapid adaptive radiation
best_support: Not established for the genus as a whole
